changes_made: Renamed k8s_rbac_audit_user_activity to k8s_audit_user_activity_monitoring for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The control AC-2 (12) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves monitoring account activities, which can be implemented using RBAC and audit logs. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but one was adjusted for clarity. The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to monitoring and auditing.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the specific action being monitored or audited.
title: Account Management | Account Monitoring for Atypical Usage
step1_reasoning: The control AC-2 (12) focuses on monitoring accounts for atypical usage, which can be mapped to monitoring and auditing user and service account activities in Kubernetes. Kubernetes RBAC and audit logs can be used to track and detect unusual access patterns or usage of service accounts and user accounts. These checks focus on ensuring that service accounts are monitored for unusual activity, user activity is audited, and any anomalous access patterns are detected through audit logs.
